claim: D28 're-entry as norm' demands a loop-control protocol, not just a recording ledger. ITERATION_LEDGER.md as currently designed records; the missing companion is backlog math (added-decisions minus resolved-decisions), oscillation detection (same decision touched 3+ times with differing values -> escalate), and livelock signaling (backlog increases 3 consecutive re-entries -> pivot-to-execute). Without it, D28 is livelock hazard. Adjacent-domain precedents unmined: Kildall fixed-point iteration, Kubernetes controller reconciliation loops, Nix eval reproducibility
evidence: OTB-7 C-140 + C-141 + C-142 structural analysis; D28 BRAINSTORM:92

claim: /migration is idempotent iff, for any unchanged (source-commit, dest-commit, plan-id) triple, a second end-to-end run produces a zero-step plan, mutates no files, emits no new LEDGER rows, and leaves the ITERATION_LEDGER decisions set unchanged. This 5-property I1-I5 contract (plan stability / zero-delta / ledger stability / reproducible derivation / level-triggered) is the core design invariant of Theme 12
evidence: G1 A1 idempotency contract; K8s reconciler rule; Terraform post-apply 0-change assertion; GV1 P1 VERIFIED Kildall + P4 VERIFIED Agent Contracts

claim: Append-only monotone ITERATION_LEDGER is the backbone of a termination proof for the brainstorm/deep-research/deep-plan triad, not just a recording convenience. Kildall/Kam-Ullman (1973/1977) prove that iterative processes on a monotone lattice of finite height converge to a fixed point. Append-only = monotone; closed decision-kind ontology = finite height. Without both, the loop can ascend forever
evidence: G1 A2; GV1 P1 VERIFIED (Wikipedia Data-flow analysis: Monotonicity ensures... finite height ensures... we will ultimately reach T(x)=x); D11 meta-ledger already mandates append-only shape

claim: Closed decision-kind ontology is required for Kildall finite-height guarantee. Finite height applies to the DECISION-KIND ontology (closed tagged enum), NOT to decision instances (D1..Dn grow unboundedly but are bounded per-run by depth limit L1). Add /migration/DECISION_KINDS.md enumerating tagged kinds; new kinds require explicit ontology bump, not silent creation
evidence: G1 A3; GV1 inconsistency #3 clarified kind-vs-instance distinction — G1 text elided it; formal framing still works if state = SET of instances drawn from finite kind-set

claim: Phase 5 MUST be level-triggered (re-read full current state each invocation), NOT edge-triggered (dispatch on diff since last iteration). Edge-triggered loops lose events and silently diverge; level-triggered self-heal on next pass. Requeue markers in .migration-state/ are progress logs, not inputs to the next reconcile
evidence: G1 A4; GV1 P2 PARTIAL — operational semantics correct, K8s main docs citation weak; lean on Kubebuilder [81] + Chainguard [83] as primary sources, not K8s main docs [80]

claim: Stage2-fixpoint acceptance (G1 A5) extends self-dogfood C6 (round-trip) + adds C8 (/migration ports /migration v_next). Scoped to v1.1, not v1, because C6 is already deferred per dispute-resolutions-arch Dispute 2. The rustc/GHC stage2 analogue is conceptually correct but empirically fragile — rustc 1.54.0 demonstrably fails byte-identity; G1's 'strongest self-hosting acceptance signal' overstated, softened to 'one of the strongest, though fragile in practice'
evidence: G1 A5; GV1 P3 CONFLICTED — rustc-dev-guide says libraries ought to be identical (not must be); G1 in-body quote mis-attributes stage1↔stage2 vs stage2↔stage3; reproducibility breaks routinely surface non-determinism

claim: 5-layer termination guard stack is required (not optional) for /migration: L1 depth (max 3 brainstorm→research→plan cycles, heuristic), L2 iterations (max 50 sub-agent dispatches per phase), L3 budget cap ($10 default), L4 wall-clock timeout (45min per phase / 4h per run), L5 circuit breaker on repeated failure (3 consecutive failed verdicts → halt). Layered, not single-guard — any one can halt, all must pass for completion
evidence: G1 A6; GV1 P4 VERIFIED Agent Contracts arXiv 2601.08815 (Ye & Tan, COINE/AAMAS 2026; 90% token reduction, 525x lower variance in iterative workflows); bounded-recursion literature 5-guardrail pattern; $47K eleven-day recursive-clarification loop cautionary tale

claim: L1 max 3 cycles in the 5-layer guard stack is an engineering heuristic, NOT a Kildall-derived bound. Kildall's formal bound is O(height × |nodes|) where height is lattice height and |nodes| is decision count — realistic /migration runs likely in the dozens. '3' is an engineering safety net for when the monotone-lattice invariant is violated, not a mathematically motivated cap
evidence: GV1 inconsistency #4 — juxtaposition of 3 next to Kildall formal bound misleading; G1 itself notes L1 is an engineering safety net

claim: Plan files (MIGRATION_PLAN.md) should carry a reproducible-derivation header: {source_sha, dest_sha, /migration_version, tool_versions, plan_hash}. Two runs with identical headers MUST produce identical plan bodies (Nix derivation analogue). Byte-identity check across runs is the trivial idempotency test
evidence: G1 A7; Nix referential transparency (Zero-to-Nix, Dolstra ICFP 2008); Reproducible Builds project

claim: A8 Plan-stability self-audit (renamed per GV1 from 'self-audit byte-identity' to 'forward-direction I1 test'). scripts/skills/migration/self-audit.js runs /migration --plan twice on frozen (source-SHA, dest-SHA) fixture and asserts byte-identity of generated PLAN.md. This is the v1-feasible zero-drift idempotency check (C7-adjacent) and is DISTINCT from A5's round-trip stage2-fixpoint (which is v1.1). Rename prevents future reader from conflating the two tests

claim: Diverse Double-Compiling (Wheeler ACSAC 2005) is the trusting-trust mitigation for /migration self-hosting: port CAS via /migration primary path, port CAS manually by a second operator secondary path, diff the two — byte-identical = DDC-cleared. Overkill for v1; belongs in /migration v2 roadmap as second-operator validation. Prevents a buggy /migration v1 producing a clean-looking ported CAS that carries the original bug's fingerprint

claim: The ideal /migration plan for a given (source, dest) pair is the LEAST FIXED POINT of iterated brainstorm∘research∘plan on a lattice, starting from ⊥ = empty ledger. Each re-entry is one step up the Kleene chain sup{f^n(⊥) | n ∈ ℕ}. Observable test: run /migration twice; if second run's LEDGER rowcount delta is zero and plan file is byte-identical, the loop has converged. Direct analogue of Terraform's second apply = 0 changes idempotency assertion
evidence: G1 fixed-point semantics; Knaster-Tarski theorem; Scott-continuous functions on complete partial orders; HashiCorp Terraform Issue #35534

claim: Grep scope-narrowness is a systemic methodology failure mode observed twice this /deep-research run — V3 (CAS auth verification: whole-CAS claim corrected to narrow scripts-layer via broader grep across skill bodies) and G2 (SoNash cost: .claude/-only grep under-reported counts vs full-repo). Identical failure mode: D/G-agent greps the subdirectory where they expected the pattern; verifier greps whole repo with same/variant patterns and catches concentrations D-agent never reached. Pattern-scope narrowness compounds with directory-scope narrowness. Recommendation for future /deep-research: verifier prompts should mandate grep broader scope AND different pattern than the D-agent's evidence path as default rule
evidence: V3 CAS auth scope correction in dispute-resolutions-cas Dispute 1 + challenges/contrarian.md Challenge 2; GV2 counts-correction on TDMS/home-context/Firestore; both instances caught by broader-scope verifier grepping

claim: Contrarian-challenger adversarial mandate is what consistently caught grep scope-narrowness failures in this run. Contrarian explicitly looks for what's outside the D-agent's frame. V-agents should be given the same adversarial mandate by default in future /deep-research runs — not just spot-check these claims but find what the D-agent didn't look at. Symmetric mirror for count-based claims: any D-agent claim of N hits across M files should be re-grepped by verifier with (a) broader directory scope AND (b) at least two pattern variants; if V-count is >1.5x D-count, flag as scope-narrowness under-report
